title: Multi-Conditional Routing with np.select()
naive_code:
# Nested slow np.where calls
out = np.where(arr < 0, -1, np.where(arr == 0, 0, 1))
wizard_code:
# 1-Liner Clean Multi-Condition Map
conditions = [arr < 0, arr == 0, arr > 0]
choices = [-1, 0, 1]
out = np.select(conditions, choices, default=99)
hinglish_explanation: Multiple levels of nested `np.where` arrays ko unreadable bana dete hain. `np.select(conditions, choices)` clean flat logic structures provide karta hai!
ai_prompt: Demonstrate numpy.select() usage with complex logical conditions for numeric arrays classification.

title: Vectorized Ternary Mapping with np.where()
naive_code:
# Loop updating array cells individually based on threshold
out = arr.copy()
for i in range(len(arr)):
    out[i] = 1 if arr[i] > 5 else 0
wizard_code:
# Instant Vectorized Choice Selection
out = np.where(arr > 5, 1, 0)
hinglish_explanation: Array elements replacement loop ki jagah `np.where(condition, true_val, false_val)` run karo—pure C-speed execution!
ai_prompt: Compare numpy.where() performance against python list comprehensions for large arrays.

title: Fast Side-by-Side Array Merging with np.c_ & np.r_
naive_code:
# Verbose concat and column expansion syntax
combined = np.concatenate((arr1.reshape(-1, 1), arr2.reshape(-1, 1)), axis=1)
wizard_code:
# Clean Shorthand Concatenation shorthand
combined = np.c_[arr1, arr2]  # Columns merging
stacked = np.r_[arr1, arr2]   # Rows merging
hinglish_explanation: Arrays stacking configurations (rows/columns combinations) shorthand translation `np.c_[a, b]` (column) aur `np.r_[a, b]` (row) 1-liner provides!
ai_prompt: Explain how numpy np.c_ and np.r_ translate slice notations to concatenation calls.

title: Einstein Summation dot operations via np.einsum()
wizard_code:
# 1-Pass Einstein Notation Summation
total = np.einsum('ij,ji->', A, B)
hinglish_explanation: `np.einsum('ij,ji->', A, B)` subscript notation use karke C-level optimized matrix operations (traces, dot products) without memory copies runs karta hai!
ai_prompt: Show how to express matrix transpose, dot product, and outer product using numpy.einsum().

title: Memory-Efficient Matrix Views vs Copy
naive_code:
# Duplicating array slice creating new memory block
sub_arr = arr[::2].copy()
wizard_code:
# Zero-Copy Array View
sub_arr = arr[::2]  # Modifying sub_arr alters original arr reference
hinglish_explanation: Slicing without `.copy()` ek view create karta hai jo same memory address share karta hai. Zero memory allocate hoti hai, par values change karne par original updates update ho jaate hain!
ai_prompt: Show how numpy shares_memory() checks if two arrays share memory buffers.

title: Piecewise Mathematical Routing via np.piecewise()
wizard_code:
# Vectorized Step Function Mapping
out = np.piecewise(arr, [arr < 0, (arr >= 0) & (arr < 5)], [0, lambda x: x**2, 25])
hinglish_explanation: Step functions (e.g. piece-by-piece continuous logic math ranges) implement karne ke liye `np.piecewise()` clean function routing provide karta hai!
ai_prompt: Demonstrate piecewise mapping in numpy for creating activation functions.

title: Coordinate Grid Matrices Creation via np.meshgrid()
wizard_code:

xx, yy = np.meshgrid(x_vals, y_vals)
hinglish_explanation: 2D Spatial coordinates grids (plots contour overlays, dynamic grids coordinate pairs) `np.meshgrid()` vectors calculations 1 line me kar deta hai!
ai_prompt: Show meshgrid mapping coordinates for contour plots and 3D surface graphing.

title: Solve Systems of Linear Equations via np.linalg.solve()
naive_code:
# Slow manual matrix inversion calculations
inv_A = np.linalg.inv(A)
x = np.dot(inv_A, b)
wizard_code:
# High Performance Numerical Solver
x = np.linalg.solve(A, b)  # Uses LAPACK solver underneath
hinglish_explanation: Systems equations check math ($Ax = b$) solve karne ke liye matrix inversion compute mat karo. `np.linalg.solve(A, b)` fast numerical analysis performs!
ai_prompt: Show why numpy.linalg.solve is numerically more stable than matrix inversion using linalg.inv.

title: Numerical Gradient Computation via np.gradient()
naive_code:
# Loop calculating adjacent differences manually
diff = np.zeros_like(arr)
for i in range(1, len(arr)-1):
    diff[i] = (arr[i+1] - arr[i-1]) / 2.0
wizard_code:
# Central Differences Numerical Gradient
dy = np.gradient(arr)
hinglish_explanation: Data distribution rates changes slope variations calculation (Numerical Derivative) central difference approximation `np.gradient()` se 1 line me calculate ho jata hai!
ai_prompt: Explain numerical gradient calculation with central differences in numpy.

title: In-Place Vector operations to save RAM allocation
naive_code:
# Allocating new temporary intermediate arrays in memory
arr = arr * 2 + 5
wizard_code:
# In-Place Operations preserving memory buffers
np.multiply(arr, 2, out=arr)
np.add(arr, 5, out=arr)
hinglish_explanation: Large arrays operations me standard operators memory duplicate check runtime spike trigger karte hain. `out=arr` parameters target modifications inplace save karte hain!
ai_prompt: Show performance and memory differences of numpy inplace out parameter usage.

title: Check Null Values presence in Vector via np.isnan()

wizard_code:
# 1-Liner Vectorized Null Check

ai_prompt: Demonstrate isnan validation variations for filter operations.

title: O(N) Top K Elements Selection via np.argpartition()
